data.title: Citigroup rises after Q4 results
data.description:
** Citigroup's shares rise 1.7% to $118.30 premarket

** Co's adjusted Q4 profit per share of $1.81 beats Street estimates of $1.67 - data compiled by LSEG

** C takes $1.2 billion charge tied to sale of Russia business to Renaissance Capital, which was approved by its board last month

** Stock rose over 65% in 2025

data.title: Jyske Bank Shares Jump on Earnings Beat
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

Jyske Bank shares jumped after the Danish bank said it expects full-year earnings to land above guidance.

Shares were 6.8% higher in afternoon Copenhagen trade.

The company had guided to net profit of between 4.9 billion and 5.3 billion Danish kroner ($763.5 million-$825.8 million) in 2025, but said in an earnings pre-release Wednesday that it now expects it ended the year with net profit at 5.41 billion kroner.

Earnings per share for the year is expected at 85 kroner versus guidance of between 77 to 84 kroner.

"The improved outlook is driven by a broadly based positive development, supported by a high level of activity and favorable financial markets," the bank said in a statement.

Fourth-quarter core earnings landed at 1.83 billion kroner, which is 10% ahead of consensus, while adjusted for one-offs in net interest income and costs the beat is 20%, UBS analyst Johan Ekblom said in a note.

Ekblom said the beat is driven by strong revenue generation and lower expenses than expected when adjusted for one-offs.

"While we would be cautious in fully extrapolating the Q4 trends before seeing the full details due on February 5, we...see scope for consensus EPS upgrades," he said.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: ImmunityBio, Inc. Announces Accelerated Approval of ANKTIVA® by Saudi FDA
data.description:
On January 14, 2026, ImmunityBio, Inc. announced that the Saudi FDA granted accelerated approval for ANKTIVA® in two cancer treatments.

Key Highlights:

  • ANKTIVA® approved for metastatic non-small cell lung cancer with checkpoint inhibitors.
  • Approval marks first global subcutaneous IL-15 receptor superagonist for lung cancer.
  • ANKTIVA® plus BCG approved for BCG-unresponsive non-muscle invasive bladder cancer.
  • ImmunityBio to open a regional office in Saudi Arabia for Middle East expansion.
  • Partnership with Biopharma Cigalah to support regional distribution and access.

Original SEC Filing: ImmunityBio, Inc. [ IBRX ] - 8-K - Jan. 14, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: US Current Account Deficit Narrows More than Expected
data.description:
The United States current account deficit narrowed by $22.8 billion, or 9.2%, to $226.4 billion in the third quarter of 2025, more than market expectations of a $238 billion deficit.

The reduction in the gap was mostly due to a narrowing in the goods deficit to $267.4 billion from $270.4 billion after firms pared back on imports after front-loading foreign purchases to dodge aggressive tariffs passed by the US Presidential Administration.

In the meantime, the services surplus expanded to $89.2 billion from $80.6 billion.

The primary account swung to a surplus of $5.2 billion from a deficit of $5.8 billion and the secondary income deficit narrowed slightly to $53.5 billion from $53.6 billion.

data.title: US Retail Sales Rebound
data.description:
Retail sales in the US increased 0.6% month-over-month in November 2025, rebounding from a revised 0.1% fall in October and better than forecasts of a 0.4% rise.

Biggest increases were seen in sales for sporting goods, hobby, musical instrument, & book stores (1.9%); miscellaneous store retailers (1.7%); gasoline stations (1.4%); building material and garden equipment suppliers (1.3%); and motor vehicle & parts dealers (1%)

data.title: US-listed shares of Trip.com fall after Chinese probe over suspected monopoly
data.description:
** U.S.-listed shares of Chinese online travel company Trip.com slip 15% to $64.16 premarket

** China's market regulator launches investigation into co over alleged monopolistic practices

** TCOM is suspected of exploiting its dominant market position, State Administration for Market Regulation says in its statement

** Chinese anti-monopoly law allows regulators to impose fines between 1% to 10% of its previous year's annual revenue for violations

** TCOM said it was "actively" co-operating with investigation and would "fully implement regulatory requirements"

** 29 of 32 brokerages rate stock "buy" or higher, three "hold"; their median PT is $87.5 - LSEG data

** TCOM has gained 5.2% in 2026

data.title: Airbnb Appoints Former Meta AI Executive CTO
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

Airbnb named Ahmad Al-Dahle, a former leader of AI at Meta, as the company's new chief technology officer.

Al-Dahle led generative AI at Meta, where he headed the team behind Meta's Llama AI models. He also previously worked at Apple, helping to develop the iPhone's display and multitouch systems and the AI systems for the company's self-driving car project.

Airbnb said the addition of Al-Dahle would help it "shape how people travel, live, and interact with AI in a way that strengthens human connection."

"He connects big ideas with technical depth, highly values design, and believes engineering should be a true strategic partner in everything we do," said Chief Executive Brian Chesky.

Airbnb is currently implementing AI agents into its business to improve customer service, personalize recommendations and facilitate trip planning.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.
